mission.title: wasmedge-runtime: bug: uninstaller not working when the `.bash_profile` is not a regular file.
mission.description: bug: uninstaller not working when the `.bash_profile` is not a regular file.. This issue affects 1+ users.

steps.title: Identify wasmedge-runtime troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your wasmedge-runtime deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n wasmedge-runtime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmedge-runtime
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmedge-runtime -n wasmedge-runtime --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review wasmedge-runtime configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant wasmedge-runtime configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n wasmedge-runtime -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmedge-runtime
kubectl get configmap -n wasmedge-runtime -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=wasmedge-runtime
```
Please check this issue:
https://github.com/second-state/LlamaEdge/issues/100

Someone is running the `installer`. However, it will run the uninstaller first to remove the previous installation. Then, it encountered the bug.

steps.title: Apply the fix for bug: uninstaller not working when the `.bash_profile` is
steps.description:
This PR fixes the uninstaller script to handle cases where the `.bash_profile` is not a regular file.

### Changes
- Checks if `.bash_profile` is a symbolic link and informs the user to update it manually.
- Adjusts the `sed` command for macOS compatibility.
- Informs the user to update manually if the file is neither regular nor symbolic.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm bug: uninstaller not working when the is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=wasmedge-runtime -n wasmedge-runtime --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n wasmedge-runtime --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
This PR fixes the uninstaller script to handle cases where the `.bash_profile` is not a regular file.

### Changes
- Checks if `.bash_profile` is a symbolic link and informs the user to update it manually.
- Adjusts the `sed` command for macOS compatibility.
- Informs the user to update manually if the file is neither regular nor symbolic.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with wasmedge-runtime installed or the issue environment reproducible.
